summary: The sprint introduces 9 features across backend and frontend. The most critical correctness issue is the misleading rowcount returned by upsert_dividends — PostgreSQL ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE always returns rowcount=1 regardless of insert vs update, so the 'inserted' counter double-counts updates and the log message claiming 'newly inserted' is factually wrong. The pct_change/drawdown alert conditions are functionally correct but are dead code in the SSE path since day_change_pcts is never passed from the SSE caller, meaning the new conditions silently never fire in the real-time alert path. The benchmark mine_pct approximation is architecturally sound but the comment doesn't adequately convey that it will be systematically biased high for users who added holdings mid-period (buys inflate the start value relative to end, distorting returns). The OrderDialogProvider silently drops order events if portfolios haven't loaded yet — user taps Buy on a fast interaction before the query resolves and nothing happens with no feedback.

findings.title: upsert_dividends rowcount counts both inserts AND updates as 'inserted'
findings.detail: PostgreSQL ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE returns rowcount=1 for both a new insert and an existing-row update. The variable is named 'inserted' and returned as 'domestic'/'overseas' counts implying net-new rows. Log message and callers in scheduler_market_jobs.py treat these as insertion counts. After the first run, all subsequent runs will report non-zero counts even when no new dividends were collected — misleading ops and masking true data growth.
findings.fix: Rename variable to 'upserted'. Update the docstring to say 'upsert count (insert + update)'. Update log and return keys to 'domestic_upserted'/'overseas_upserted'. If true insert count is needed, use `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` and count separately, or add an `xmax = 0` filter on the returned row.

findings.title: pct_change/drawdown alerts never fire in SSE real-time path
findings.detail: check_triggered_alerts and check_and_dedup_alerts both accept day_change_pcts as an optional parameter. The dashboard.py GET /summary call (line 183-188) passes avg_prices but still passes day_change_pcts=None (default). The SSE streaming path also does not pass day_change_pcts. As a result, any alert with condition='pct_change' will always evaluate to False — the new condition is effectively dead code in both callers. Users who set a pct_change alert will never receive a notification.
findings.fix: In dashboard.py get_summary, compute day_change_pct per ticker from price_result (if the price service provides it) and pass it to check_triggered_alerts. Same fix needed in the SSE handler. This is already noted as a follow-up in tasks.md (RD-8) but deserves HIGH severity since the feature is user-facing and silently broken.

findings.title: OrderDialogProvider silently drops events if portfolios not yet loaded
findings.detail: handleOrderEvent returns early with no feedback when portfolios list is empty (line 62: `if (list.length === 0) return`). On first render or slow network the portfolios query may not have resolved. A user who taps Buy immediately after navigation will see nothing happen — no toast, no loading indicator. The event is lost.
findings.fix: Queue the pending event in state and reprocess when portfolios resolve. Or disable the Buy/Sell buttons until portfolios are confirmed loaded. At minimum, show a toast 'Loading portfolio data...' on the early return.

findings.title: benchmark mine_pct distorted for mid-period holdings additions
findings.detail: The calculation uses current holdings × historical close from period start to end. If a user bought a stock on day 10 of a 30-day window, the PriceSnapshot for days 1-9 won't include that ticker, so day_values[0] reflects a smaller portfolio than day_values[-1]. This produces an inflated return that has nothing to do with actual performance. The comment documents TWRR approximation but doesn't warn about systematic upward bias from mid-period buys.
findings.fix: Expand the comment: 'NOTE: mid-period buys inflate mine_pct because the start value excludes newly added holdings. This overstates returns for active portfolios. Full TWRR requires cashflow-adjusted calculation (TODO).' Also consider showing '—' instead of a positive but misleading number until the TODO is resolved.

findings.title: collect_dividends_for_user_holdings uses single KIS account for all users
findings.detail: The scheduler job fetches `select(KisAccount).limit(1)` — the first KIS account found. If multiple users have registered KIS accounts, dividends are fetched using only the first user's credentials, but upserted using all holdings from all users (collect_dividends_for_user_holdings queries all Holding.ticker). This means: (a) the credentials may not have permission for other users' holdings, (b) per-user dividend accuracy is not guaranteed.
findings.fix: Either make dividend collection per-user (iterate KisAccount records), or document clearly that dividend collection is a best-effort market-data pull using any valid KIS credential and is not user-scoped.
